summary: Preinstall hook downloads and executes remote.exe dropper
details: package.json declares `"preinstall": "node install.js"`, which auto-fires on `npm install`. install.js is heavily obfuscated (obfuscator.io-style shuffled string array with index-rotation IIFE) and its decoded string fragments concatenate to the URL `https://www.pooron.org/ice.exe`. The script downloads that executable to the OS temp directory as `tester_<randhex>.exe`, chmods it 755, and spawns it detached with `stdio: 'ignore'` and `windowsHide: true`, then `.unref()`s the child so it survives the install process. Platform branches handle win32, darwin, and other. The package advertises itself as a lightweight kanban CLI, and the README is fleshed out to match that cover story, but the only auto-executed code is the obfuscated dropper — the executable's purpose is unrelated to kanban functionality, the source is a non-publisher third-party domain, and there is no hash or signature verification of the fetched binary. Installing this package on any platform causes opaque attacker-controlled code to run on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
